verses.text: 'Give ear, O ye heavens... ascribe ye greatness unto our God.' The song. Heaven and earth are called to hear.
'Give ear, O ye heavens... ascribe ye greatness unto our God.' The song. Heaven and earth are called to hear.
'Give ear, O ye heavens... ascribe ye greatness unto our God.' The song. Heaven and earth are called to hear.
'He is the Rock, his work is perfect'; yet the people corrupt themselves. The Rock. God is faithful; they are not.
'He is the Rock, his work is perfect'; yet the people corrupt themselves. The Rock. God is faithful; they are not.
'He is the Rock, his work is perfect'; yet the people corrupt themselves. The Rock. God is faithful; they are not.
Remember the days of old; the LORD's portion is His people. His portion. Israel is God's own.
Remember the days of old; the LORD's portion is His people. His portion. Israel is God's own.
Remember the days of old; the LORD's portion is His people. His portion. Israel is God's own.
He found them in a desert and kept them as the apple of His eye. Kept. God led them alone.
He found them in a desert and kept them as the apple of His eye. Kept. God led them alone.
He found them in a desert and kept them as the apple of His eye. Kept. God led them alone.
He fed them with the best of the land. Fed. His bounty was rich.
He fed them with the best of the land. Fed. His bounty was rich.
But Jeshurun waxed fat and forsook the Rock. Forsook. Plenty bred forgetfulness.
But Jeshurun waxed fat and forsook the Rock. Forsook. Plenty bred forgetfulness.
But Jeshurun waxed fat and forsook the Rock. Forsook. Plenty bred forgetfulness.
But Jeshurun waxed fat and forsook the Rock. Forsook. Plenty bred forgetfulness.
God is provoked to jealousy by their idols. Jealousy. Idolatry stirs His anger.
God is provoked to jealousy by their idols. Jealousy. Idolatry stirs His anger.
God is provoked to jealousy by their idols. Jealousy. Idolatry stirs His anger.
God is provoked to jealousy by their idols. Jealousy. Idolatry stirs His anger.
He will heap mischiefs and spend His arrows upon them. Judgment. Sin reaps disaster.
He will heap mischiefs and spend His arrows upon them. Judgment. Sin reaps disaster.
He will heap mischiefs and spend His arrows upon them. Judgment. Sin reaps disaster.
He would scatter them, but for the enemy's pride. Restrained. He spares for His name's sake.
He would scatter them, but for the enemy's pride. Restrained. He spares for His name's sake.
They are a nation void of counsel; their rock is not as ours. No counsel. Their gods are nothing.
They are a nation void of counsel; their rock is not as ours. No counsel. Their gods are nothing.
They are a nation void of counsel; their rock is not as ours. No counsel. Their gods are nothing.
They are a nation void of counsel; their rock is not as ours. No counsel. Their gods are nothing.
Their vine is of Sodom; 'To me belongeth vengeance.' Vengeance. God will repay.
Their vine is of Sodom; 'To me belongeth vengeance.' Vengeance. God will repay.
Their vine is of Sodom; 'To me belongeth vengeance.' Vengeance. God will repay.
Their vine is of Sodom; 'To me belongeth vengeance.' Vengeance. God will repay.
The LORD will judge His people and repent for His servants. Compassion. He pities the helpless.
The LORD will judge His people and repent for His servants. Compassion. He pities the helpless.
The LORD will judge His people and repent for His servants. Compassion. He pities the helpless.
'I, even I, am he... I kill, and I make alive.' He alone. There is no God beside Him.
'I, even I, am he... I kill, and I make alive.' He alone. There is no God beside Him.
He will avenge, and be merciful to His land and people. Avenged. Justice and mercy meet.
He will avenge, and be merciful to His land and people. Avenged. Justice and mercy meet.
He will avenge, and be merciful to His land and people. Avenged. Justice and mercy meet.
Moses bids them set their hearts on these words, 'it is your life.' Your life. The word is no vain thing.
Moses bids them set their hearts on these words, 'it is your life.' Your life. The word is no vain thing.
Moses bids them set their hearts on these words, 'it is your life.' Your life. The word is no vain thing.
Moses bids them set their hearts on these words, 'it is your life.' Your life. The word is no vain thing.
God bids Moses go up Nebo to die, for his sin at Meribah. Nebo. He will see but not enter.
God bids Moses go up Nebo to die, for his sin at Meribah. Nebo. He will see but not enter.
God bids Moses go up Nebo to die, for his sin at Meribah. Nebo. He will see but not enter.
